review_comment:
(note for anybody that comes along)
I was curious how this would affect the `ToJson()`. Luckily, `GlobalAppSettings::ToJson()` doesn't look at the parent. So we don't ever serialize this parent globals object.

That said, I can't (at least immediately) figure out why you're able to access these actions. ActionMap still only has one parent. So what's the flow, exactly?

I'm not sure how to answer the "what's the flow" question--all of the settings stuff is kind of a twisty maze to me. This was an approach suggested by lhecker, as a way for a global bool to enable a bunch of actions. And it's why the asserts in ActionMap.cpp are removed: if you have `EnableColorSelection` set to "true", those asserts would fire.
